description: Implement the WhatsApp connection service using Baileys + baileys-antiban with pairing code authentication. This handles connecting user WhatsApp numbers, managing sessions, and exposing a message-sending API.
steps: Create server/src/services/whatsapp.service.ts - WhatsAppService class that manages multiple Baileys socket connections. Methods: initConnection(userId, phoneNumber) - creates a new Baileys socket with baileys-antiban wrapSocket, requests pairing code and returns it. confirmConnection(userId, phoneNumber) - marks session as connected once connection event fires. disconnectSession(userId, sessionId). getSessionStatus(userId, sessionId). sendMessage(userId, sessionId, to, message).

In whatsapp.service.ts, configure Baileys with: printQRInTerminal: false (we use pairing code), auth state stored per session using useMultiFileAuthState from baileys in SESSION_PATH/<userId>/<phoneNumber>/. Wrap socket with wrapSocket from baileys-antiban for anti-ban protection.

In whatsapp.service.ts, handle connection events: on 'connection.update' - detect open/close states, update session status in database. On 'creds.update' - save credentials. Handle reconnection logic with exponential backoff.

Create server/src/routes/whatsapp.ts - POST /api/whatsapp/connect (phoneNumber) - validates user tier allows another number, calls initConnection, returns pairing code. GET /api/whatsapp/sessions - list user's connected sessions with status. DELETE /api/whatsapp/sessions/:id - disconnect and remove session. GET /api/whatsapp/sessions/:id/status - get real-time status of a session.

Create server/src/routes/messages.ts - POST /api/messages/send (sessionId, to, message) - validates session belongs to user and is connected, checks rate limit, sends message via whatsapp service. GET /api/messages/history (sessionId, limit, offset) - basic message log.
Create server/src/db/migrations.ts - Add messages_log table: id, user_id, session_id, to_number, message_type, content, status, created_at. Track sent messages for rate limiting and history.
Mount the new routes in server/src/index.ts behind the auth middleware. WhatsApp and message routes require authentication.
Create server/src/utils/validators.ts - Phone number validation (E.164 format), message content validation, session ownership validation helpers.
Add tier enforcement in the connect route: check how many active sessions user has vs their tier limit before allowing a new connection.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/whatsapp/connect with a valid phone number returns a pairing code string
Free tier users cannot connect more than 1 WhatsApp number
Pro tier users can connect up to 5 numbers
Sessions are persisted to filesystem and survive server restart
POST /api/messages/send validates session ownership and connection status before sending
Rate limiting correctly tracks and enforces daily message limits per tier
The server compiles and builds without TypeScript errors
